memory.summary: I have a free afternoon but am barely started on my Thursday deadline, though I could quickly finish the easy chore or relax now.
I made a small start on the easy chore, but my low energy and sleep debt mean I need to be careful with my remaining time before the Thursday deadline.
I made small progress on the easy chore, but my low energy and sleep debt still limit my capacity before the Thursday deadline.
I made slight progress on the easy chore, but the afternoon is slipping away while the main project remains untouched.
I made progress on the term project, but realizing that future procrastination compounds stress highlights the need to prioritize this work over low-impact tasks.
I made slight progress on the easy chore, but my low energy and sleep debt still limit my capacity before the Thursday deadline.
I made slight progress on the easy chore, but this low-impact task continues to consume time while my main project remains untouched and stress rises.
The easy chore is still consuming time with minimal progress, confirming that prioritizing it over the term project increases stress and risks missing the Thursday deadline.
The trivial chore continues to yield negligible progress while draining my limited energy and increasing stress, confirming it is a poor use of time before the Thursday deadline.
Even after 0.5 hours, the trivial chore only reached 30% completion while my energy remains low and stress high, confirming this task is a poor use of time before Thursday.
After 0.5 hours, the trivial chore barely advanced to 31%, confirming it drains energy and increases stress without meaningful progress before Thursday's deadline.
Despite another half-hour of effort, the trivial chore only reached 33% completion with under an hour left before its deadline, confirming it remains a high-stress, low-yield activity.
